summary: Patrick McKenzie reports his best month ever for Bingo Card Creator, with 30 sales generating $760 gross and $673 net profit in March 2007. He notes that accidentally disabling CD orders cost him significant sales, and gives himself 50-50 odds of hitting $1,000 in April. AdWords is performing well at $0.34 per demo download, while SEO gains are bringing organic links from school districts directly to his landing pages. The post also celebrates his blog hitting 100,000 page views and thanks his community of readers.
key_points: March 2007 was his best month ever with $673 net profit on 30 sales, despite accidentally disabling CD orders for most of the month
AdWords cost efficiency reached near-best levels at $0.34 per demo download, but the $24.95 price point limits how aggressively he can bid on keywords
School districts are organically linking to his AdWords landing pages, providing both qualified traffic and SEO authority — the ideal combination of content marketing
His blog reached 100,000 page views and is becoming a meaningful traffic source, competing with MSN and Yahoo for third-largest source of downloads
The thin margin between ad spend and product price creates a hard ceiling on paid acquisition growth for low-priced software
money_quotes: Best month ever. Would have done better had I not disabled CD orders inadvertently for most of it.
There is not too much play in $24.95 to go crazy with keywords costing, e.g., 15 to 25 cents apiece.
They put my software in front of hundreds of prospective customers AND they give me an authority link. Content, its the gift that keeps on giving.
The majority of my readers continue to be uISVs or folks thinking about taking the plunge, but I'm getting increasing mentions from folks as diverse as librarians and customer advocates.
I'm overjoyed and humbled by your support, and couldn't do it without you.
key_insight: At low price points, the economics of paid acquisition create a hard ceiling on growth, making organic channels like SEO and content marketing disproportionately valuable.
